case_metadata.user_request: Find the lowest-energy adsorption configuration for the 2-hydroxyethyl radical on the provided surface.
case_metadata.notes: Resolved from prior AdsMind outputs; local evidence matches formula C2H5O

plan.reasoning: History shows ontop (Cu, adsorbate index 0) at -2.050 eV and bridge (Cu-Cu, adsorbate index 0) at -2.120 eV; both succeeded without dissociation. Hollow sites remain untested. SMILES [CH2]CO and RDKit data confirm ReactiveSpecies binding at radical carbon (index 0). Propose hollow site on three Cu atoms to explore lower-energy configuration on the alloy surface.

No prior attempts. SMILES [CH2]CO matches 2-hydroxyethyl radical with unpaired electron on C index 0 (SP3, radical_electrons=1). Use ReactiveSpecies binding exclusively at index 0. Start with simplest stable ontop Cu site consistent with listed sites.
